rxn_class_summary: Copper-catalyzed enantioselective intramolecular alkene carboetherification (and hydroetherification) of pentenols to form fused tetrahydrofurans via oxycupration/radical aryl addition.

key_intermediates.name: primary carbon radical from C-Cu(II) homolysis (29)

electron_flow: Cu(II)-bisoxazoline binds the alkene of the pentenol and undergoes cis-oxycupration to give an alkyl-Cu(II) THF intermediate; homolysis of the C-Cu(II) bond releases Cu(I) and a primary carbon radical, which adds to the tethered arene to give an aryl/cyclohexadienyl radical that is reoxidized (MnO2 reoxidizes Cu(I) to Cu(II)) and rearomatized to yield the fused bicyclic product.
rate_determining_step: C-C bond formation (radical addition of primary C-radical to the arene) is implicated as slow relative to H-atom abstraction by 1,4-cyclohexadiene

probe_reagents.name: 1,4-cyclohexadiene (H-atom donor)
probe_reagents.observation: Intercepts the carbon radical to give 2,3-trans tetrahydrofuran 27 in 82% yield (and 26 in 85% yield, 76% ee) instead of carboetherification product, confirming a carbon radical intermediate and that HAT is faster than C-C bond formation.

probe_reagents.name: Deuterium-labeled 3-p-methoxybenzyl-4-penten-1-ol (10a-D)
probe_reagents.observation: Carboetherification gave 1.8:1 trans/cis 11a-D:12a-D, each as 1:1 diastereomeric mixture at the deuterated carbon, indicating loss of alkene stereochemistry consistent with an sp2 (radical) intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: Regiochemical probe (ortho vs para arene addition)
probe_reagents.observation: Ortho-substituted product formed as major (ortho:para = 1.9:1), consistent with radical (not electrophilic/Friedel-Crafts) addition to the arene.

probe_reagents.name: Chiral Cu(OTf)2/(R,R)-Ph-Box catalyst variation
probe_reagents.observation: Enantioselectivity (up to ~75% ee carboetherification, 76% ee hydroetherification) supports Cu involvement in the alkene oxycupration step.

notes: Authors considered alternative C-C bond-forming mechanisms: (i) radical addition to arene (favored), (ii) electrophilic aromatic substitution via organocopper(III) (direct or via reductive elimination), and (iii) Friedel-Crafts on a primary carbocation (rejected as too high energy). The 2,3-trans selectivity in hydroetherification is rationalized via TS-D placing the allylic substituent pseudo-equatorial; diminished ee in carboetherification is attributed to reversible ring-opening/ring-closing of the carbon radical prior to C-C bond formation. MnO2 serves as terminal oxidant to regenerate Cu(II) from Cu(I).
